item.title: What Happens When Militancy Conflicts With Motherhood
item.description: Two “Weather kids” reckon with the unyielding political convictions that compromised their parents’ domestic duties.

item.title: The Average Guys Outsmarting Wall Street on Prediction Markets
item.description: How prediction market “sharps” have made millions wagering on everything from war to Rotten Tomatoes.

item.title: Spencer Pratt Wants to Be Mayor. His Fans Want Him to Be Batman.
item.description: What an A.I.-generated video of the former reality TV star teaches us about the future of political ads.

item.title: My Parents Suspect I’ve Lost My Faith. Should I Tell Them the Truth?
item.description: My mother sent me a long voicemail saying she believes I no longer pray, and implied that my wife may be to blame.

item.title: Not Every Language Has Spelling Bees. Here’s Why English Is Perfect for Them.
item.description: English’s hybrid history, silent letters and overcomplicated rules are what make the competition fierce.

item.title: The Politics of the Downwardly Mobile Professional Class
item.description: The debate over whether Graham Platner is “working class” comes at a time when more and more people are at risk of falling into it.

item.title: Can You Use Dirty Rubber Bands in the Kitchen?
item.description: A ruling on a cleanliness dispute.

item.title: My Family Member Is Being Pressured to Donate a Kidney. Should I Say Something?
item.description: The recipient would be his stepfather’s colleague, whom he barely knows.

item.title: The Small-Business Owners Managing Whole Armies of A.I. Employees
item.description: When you turn A.I. agents loose on your finances, email and customers, what could possibly go wrong?

item.title: Should You Outsource Your Morning Routine to a Chatbot?
item.description: A.I. wants to help you start the day. Its suggestions? Drink coffee and get dressed.

item.title: A University System Went All In on A.I. Now It’s Tearing Itself Apart.
item.description: California’s public universities spent $16.9 million on A.I. during a financial crisis, and the result has been chaos.

item.title: Could the A.I. Mattress Topper Beloved by Billionaires Help Me Get Some Sleep?
item.description: The luxury gadget promises to hack sleep — and relationships. But can anything?

item.title: Can You Get Rich Quick Off A.I. Slop?
item.description: Brainrot videos are specifically designed to confuse us. They can also be a financial bonanza for their creators.
